answer:
The sources confirm that angels are created from light and execute God's commands without question [1]. However, the provided texts do not explicitly attribute this specific detail to a hadith in Sahih al-Bukhari or Sahih Muslim (you may want to independently verify this specific attribution outside of the sources). 

Authentic narrations in the sources vividly describe the physical, unseen realities of Paradise and Hellfire, which anchor their theological study:

**The Realities of Paradise**
*   **Landscape and Scale:** Paradise features tents or walls made of pearls, and its earth is composed of musk [2]. It contains a tree so massive that a rider could travel under its shade for one hundred years without crossing it [3]. 
*   **The Inhabitants:** The first group to enter Paradise will glitter like the bright full moon [4, 5]. They will use golden combs, their sweat will smell like musk, and they will have spouses whose bone marrow is visible through their flesh due to their extreme beauty and purity [4, 5].

**The Realities of Hellfire**
*   **Intensity of the Heat:** Earthly fire is only one-seventieth as hot as Hellfire [6]. Furthermore, the severe heat of summer and the bitter cold of winter on Earth are the direct result of Hellfire being permitted by God to exhale twice a year [7, 8].
*   **Specific Torments:** Authentic narrations detail specific physical punishments for various sins. For example, adulterers will be placed in an oven-like hole, those who dealt in usury will swim in a river of blood while stones are thrown into their mouths, and liars will have their cheeks continuously torn [9-11]. 
*   **The Bridge (As-Sirat):** A bridge will be laid across Hell, equipped with massive hooks, tongs, and spits resembling the thorns of Sa'dan [12, 13]. These hooks will catch and entangle people, pulling them into the fire based on their earthly misdeeds [13].

Volume 4, Book 54, Number 469: Narrated Abu Huraira:
The Prophet said, "Fever is from the heat of the (Hell) Fire, so cool it with water."
Volume 1, Book 10, Number 511: Narrated Abu Dhar:
Volume 4, Book 54, Number 481: Narrated Abu Sad:

The man went to pick it up and by the time he returned to him, the crushed head had returned to its normal state and the man came back and struck him again (and so on). I said, 'Who is this?' They told me to proceed on; so we proceeded on and passed by a hole like an oven; with a narrow top and wide bottom, and the fire was kindling underneath that hole. Whenever the fire-flame went up, the people were lifted up to such an extent that they about to get out of it, and whenever the fire got quieter, the people went down into it, and there were naked men and women in it. I said, 'Who is this?' They told me to proceed on. So we proceeded on till we reached a river of blood and a man was in it, and another man was standing at its bank with stones in front of him, facing the man standing in the river. Whenever the man in the river wanted to come out, the other one threw a stone in his mouth and caused him to retreat to his original position; and so whenever he wanted to come out the other would throw a stone in his mouth, and he would retreat to his original position. I asked, 'What is this?' They told me to proceed on and we did so till we reached a well-flourished green garden having a huge tree and near its root was sitting an old man with some children. (I saw) An-other man near the tree with fire in front of him and he was kindling it up. Then they (i.e. my two companions) made me climb up the tree and made me enter a house, better than which I have ever seen. In it were some old men and young men, women and children.
1) God: There is only one God with no associate or partner. All that happened in the past, is happening now and is going to happen in the future is by the will of God.
